title: asperula tomentosa var. deficiens
intro: Asperula tomentosa var. deficiens, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant agent. The plant's health benefits include treating digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Asperula tomentosa var. deficiens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation, calm the nervous system, and support the immune system.
